request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Given a prediction-market contract and a single regulatory news record, decide if this record is relevant to whether the contract resolves YES or NO. Score relevance 0-10 (0 = off-topic, 10 = directly determinative). Return JSON matching the schema. one_line_why should be ≤160 chars and explain how this record moves resolution probability.
request.user:
Contract title: Will the US enter a recession in 2026?
Resolution criteria: Resolves YES if the National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) Business Cycle Dating Committee announces that the United States entered an economic recession with a peak month in 2026, on or before 2026-12-31. Resolves NO otherwise.
Conditions:
  A: NBER 2026-peak recession by 2026-12-31 — The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee publicly announces that the U.S. entered a recession with the peak month occurring in 2026, and this announcement is made on or before 2026-12-31.
Settlement entities: ['National Bureau of Economic Research', 'Federal Reserve System', 'Federal Open Market Committee', 'Department of the Treasury', 'Bureau of Labor Statistics', 'Bureau of Economic Analysis', 'Council of Economic Advisers']

Record title: Treasury, IRS issue proposed regulations on how to open initial Trump Accounts under the One, Big, Beautiful Bill
Record date: 2026-03-06
Record regulator: Department of the Treasury and the Internal Revenue Service
Record topic: Internal Revenue Service
Record entities: ['Department of the Treasury and the Internal Revenue Service', 'Frank J. Bisignano']

response.one_line_why: Treasury/IRS proposed regs on opening specific tax accounts are tax-administration details and do not meaningfully affect whether NBER will date a 2026 recession.
